factions.title: Bureau of Cognitive Integrity
factions.kicker: Public Safety Authority // Internal Control Network
factions.subtitle: Official Cognitive Safety Authority

Public Face.value: The BCI presents itself as Meridian Spire’s guardian against cognitive contamination, trauma spirals, dangerous memory instability, and illegal memory technology.

Actual Truth.value: The BCI protects the edited version of history. It uses surveillance, records manipulation, medicalized memory correction, propaganda, and tactical force to keep the city stable and obedient.

Player-Safe Read.value: Citizens know the BCI as stern but necessary. Licensed PIs know the BCI can freeze cases, revoke access, lock terminals, and make evidence disappear.

GM Warning.value: The BCI should feel lawful before it feels evil. Their scariest power is not violence; it is making everyone agree the violence was necessary.

Territory.value: Civic Core, Cognitive Health Clinics, BCI Processing Towers, patrol zones, black-site annexes, and emergency response corridors.

structure.label: Directorate
structure.value: Policy, citywide control, elite coordination, classified continuity orders.

structure.label: Internal Security
structure.value: Leak suppression, erased case files, black-bag warrants, evidence laundering.

structure.label: Patrol Division
structure.value: Street presence, checkpoint authority, PI interference, public compliance.

structure.label: Enforcer Corps
structure.value: High-risk tactical response, raids, forced extractions, memory seizure operations.

structure.label: Cognitive Health Division
structure.value: Therapeutic memory correction, compliance medicine, post-incident reconditioning.

structure.label: Public Trust Office
structure.value: Propaganda, citizen advisories, controlled crisis narratives.

npcs.name: Director Mara Veyne
npcs.body:
Director of the BCI
“Your memories are protected. Your fear is understood. Your cooperation is required.”
Public: Trusted public face of cognitive safety. Appears on citywide broadcasts after crises.
Truth: Knows memory alteration has been used politically and believes the lie is necessary to preserve Meridian Spire.
Open full dossier
Vibe: Cold, elegant, terrifyingly calm.
Secret: Has seen original massacre records and the edits that followed.
First Appearance: Broadcasts, propaganda screens, official warnings, and terminal overrides.
DM Use: Use when the party becomes a city-level problem.

npcs.name: Captain Orin Vale
npcs.body:
BCI Patrol Captain
“Licensed private investigators. Interesting. That makes this unfortunate instead of suspicious.”

Truth: Ambitious, observant, and willing to use the party as a stepping stone or case file.

Secret: Suspects BCI hides something larger than routine corruption but lacks courage to pursue it.
First Appearance: Arrives after the Session 1 apartment incident or after a terminal hack alert.
DM Use: Early recurring antagonist. Threatens licenses, scenes, witnesses, and access.

factions.title: Choir of the Unforgotten
factions.subtitle: Memory Rebels / Grief Movement / Underground Resistance

Public Face.value: The BCI describes the Choir as dangerous agitators who radicalize the grieving and spread illegal memory material.

Actual Truth.value: The Choir believes forgotten people have been murdered twice: once in life and once in memory. They fight to recover names, stories, and proof of erased lives.

Player-Safe Read.value: Citizens know graffiti, hacked screens, and whispers: “The Choir remembers.” PIs hear that they can find witnesses nobody else can.

GM Warning.value: The Choir should not be clean heroes. They are right about the crime, but desperation can make them reckless and dangerous.

Territory.value: The Dregs, runoff tunnels, slum sectors, pirate relay stations, hidden memorial rooms, abandoned civic shelters.

Secret: Helped shape early therapeutic memory correction before seeing what the system became.
First Appearance: A hacked public terminal interrupts a BCI broadcast with her voice.
DM Use: Make her right about the horror but questionable in what she will sacrifice.

factions.title: Truth Division
factions.kicker: Unregistered Truth Network // Memory Contraband Risk
factions.subtitle: Unedited History Cell / Purple-Signal Hackers

Public Face.value: Most citizens do not know Truth Division exists. BCI files describe them as conspiracy terrorists spreading corrosive historical falsehoods.

Actual Truth.value: Truth Division wants the unedited past exposed, even if the revelation destabilizes Meridian Spire. They are less emotional than CotU and less patient than the Red Archive.

Player-Safe Read.value: Players first experience them through purple flashes, terminal warnings, and messages like “you aren’t ready.”

GM Warning.value: Truth Division should be allies who make the party nervous. They value truth more than comfort, safety, or timing.

Territory.value: Hidden servers, dead terminals, ghost forums, compromised civic relays, old academic networks, abandoned data vaults.

Public Face.value: Officially, the Red Archive does not exist. Unofficially, people whisper that someone kept the records the city tried to bury.

Actual Truth.value: The Red Archive preserves forbidden records, erased names, and pre-edited history. They believe truth is a weapon and should not be handed to everyone.

Player-Safe Read.value: Players may first encounter the Archive through a refined field agent, impossible document access, or a red mark left at a scene.

GM Warning.value: The Archive is not heroic. They protect truth by controlling it. They may help the party while still manipulating them.

Territory.value: Hidden archives, locked museums, abandoned libraries, private vaults, gallery fronts, data mausoleums.

factions.title: Continuance Initiative
factions.kicker: Private Research Consortium // Cognitive Continuity Program
factions.subtitle: Neural Continuity / Memory Transfer / Elite Survival Science

Public Face.value: Continuance claims to treat neurological decay, trauma loss, identity collapse, and memory deterioration through advanced continuity research.

Actual Truth.value: Continuance is one of the few groups that knows memory transfer is real. Its leadership is obsessed with preserving identity beyond death, especially for the powerful.

Player-Safe Read.value: To the public, Continuance sounds hopeful and humane. To people who dig too deep, it feels like death wearing a lab coat.

GM Warning.value: Continuance should make the party ask whether survival without the original body is mercy, theft, or a copy pretending to be a person.

Territory.value: Corporate labs, private hospitals, sealed research floors, donor clinics, prototype containment, elite gala spaces.

factions.kicker: Information Contraband Network // Organized Data Crime
factions.subtitle: Information Economy / Criminal Logistics / Red Thread Underworld

Public Face.value: The Grey Market is not one organization; it is the illegal economy of secrets. RedWire is one of the more feared cartels inside that economy.

Actual Truth.value: Information is currency in Meridian Spire. Grey Market brokers buy, steal, launder, and sell data, memory shards, identities, blackmail, surveillance, and BCI tech.

Player-Safe Read.value: The party will need the Grey Market when official channels fail. The cost may be money, favors, reputation, or old secrets.

GM Warning.value: This faction works best when it is useful and awful at the same time. Let players like these NPCs, then remind them everyone has a price.

Territory.value: Back rooms, nightclubs, market dens, monorail maintenance tunnels, runoff routes, private clinics, abandoned data shops.

Public Face.value: A missing childhood friend from the old school group. Publicly, his family disappearance became one more unresolved case Meridian Spire learned to stop discussing.

The Truth.value: Damien was the emotional center of Ash, Colton, and Mozzie’s childhood circle. He found or inherited information tied to his family, the 2074 BCI case, and altered records, then tried to pass it on before he was silenced.

Vibe.value: Tragic, nervous, desperate; a normal person pushed into bravery.

Why They Matter.value: He is the first thread that pulls the party back to the case that broke their childhood: Damien’s disappearance, the Ruiz family silence, and the question of what Colton, Ash, and Mozzie each remember differently.

Secret.value: Damien may not have been the real target. The BCI may have been watching whoever came to collect what he had.

First Encounter.value: A destroyed apartment, a broken memory shard container, a wounded survivor, and a still-warm portable BCI terminal.

DM Notes.value: Use Damien like the campaign’s first ghost. Every answer about him should create two more questions.

Quote.value: Licensed private investigators. Interesting. That makes this unfortunate instead of suspicious.

Public Face.value: A disciplined BCI field captain assigned to disturbances, PI overreach, illegal data activity, and urban incidents.

The Truth.value: Ambitious and observant. He sees the party as a stepping stone, a problem, or a case file waiting to happen.

Vibe.value: Professional, sharp, and irritatingly reasonable.

Why They Matter.value: He is the first recurring BCI pressure point the party can actually talk to, evade, or anger.

Secret.value: He suspects the BCI is hiding something larger than routine corruption, but he is not brave enough to ask the wrong questions yet.

First Encounter.value: Arrives after the Session 1 incident or after the terminal warning that BCI authorities are on route.

DM Notes.value: Use him to threaten licenses, interrogate witnesses, freeze scenes, and make the party feel watched.

Quote.value: I don’t care what you found. I care what you can prove without getting my office raided.

Public Face.value: A city licensing contact who handles PI permits, case permissions, paperwork, and official warnings.

The Truth.value: Dax knows some cases are cursed. He flags them because people who touch them tend to vanish.

Vibe.value: Overworked government clerk with survival instincts and a permanent headache.

Why They Matter.value: He gives legal pressure without every obstacle needing to be a BCI squad.

Secret.value: He has a private list of cases marked DO NOT TOUCH because those case numbers keep disappearing from city systems.

First Encounter.value: Calls the party after the apartment incident: their licenses are being reviewed.

DM Notes.value: Use as a leash, warning siren, and reluctant source of official access.

Quote.value: That’s not a drive. That’s a confession someone tried to make portable.

Public Face.value: A small-time dealer in old tech, black-market storage, and illegal data movement.

The Truth.value: Cricket knows enough about memory shard containers to identify who made one and who could afford it.

Vibe.value: Nervous, quick, squirrelly, useful in exactly the way that gets people killed.

Why They Matter.value: The party needs someone who can identify the broken container from Session 1.

Secret.value: Cricket has seen one container with the same seal before: it was linked to a BCI-adjacent black clinic.

First Encounter.value: Found hiding in a market stall, ready to bolt the second anyone says BCI.

DM Notes.value: Use Cricket for shard lore, illegal tech IDs, and comic relief that can turn tragic.

npcs.name: Director Mara Veyne
npcs.role: Director of the Bureau of Cognitive Integrity

Quote.value: Your memories are protected. Your fear is understood. Your cooperation is required.

Public Face.value: The trusted face of cognitive safety in Meridian Spire. Appears on official broadcasts after crises.

The Truth.value: She knows memory alteration is not only medical. She knows it has been used politically and believes the lie is necessary to preserve the city.

Vibe.value: Cold, elegant, terrifyingly calm.

Why They Matter.value: She is the face of the system itself.

Secret.value: She has seen records of the original massacre and the edits that followed. She may not have created the lie, but she maintains it.

First Encounter.value: Seen through citywide BCI broadcasts, propaganda screens, and terminal warnings before any in-person meeting.

DM Notes.value: Use when the party becomes a city-level problem.

Quote.value: End this now. Compliance is your last mercy.

Public Face.value: Commander of high-risk BCI tactical response teams. Most citizens know him only through red-alert footage.

The Truth.value: He leads black-bag raids, memory seizure operations, forced disappearances, and suppression missions.

Vibe.value: Male tactical hammer of the BCI. Minimal words, maximum pressure.

Why They Matter.value: His arrival means the campaign has escalated from investigation into survival.

Secret.value: He remembers every major raid he has led, especially the ones involving innocents. He buries guilt under obedience.

First Encounter.value: Red emergency lights, heavy boots, synchronized Enforcer units, and no negotiation posture.

DM Notes.value: Use sparingly. He should feel like a boss warning, not a routine patrol officer.

Quote.value: A disappeared file is not evidence. It is an administrative correction.

Public Face.value: Senior BCI administrator responsible for internal security and classified investigations.

The Truth.value: He handles leaks, coverups, disappearances, and political damage control.

Vibe.value: Bureaucratic monster. Can ruin a life with a form.

Why They Matter.value: He is the hidden blade behind BCI paperwork.

Secret.value: He personally approved cleanup operations tied to family disappearance cases.

First Encounter.value: His name appears as an authorization stamp on a redacted BCI report.

DM Notes.value: Use him when the party starts tracing who signed off on ruined lives.

Quote.value: The mind protects itself. We simply assist the process.

Public Face.value: A respected cognitive health specialist involved in trauma treatment and memory stabilization.

The Truth.value: He performs therapeutic memory correction that can hide abuse, erase people, and reframe history.

Vibe.value: Gentle voice, clean gloves, absolute nightmare.

Why They Matter.value: He embodies the medical mask of the BCI.

Secret.value: He has treated people who later forgot entire family members.

First Encounter.value: Interviewing a victim who suddenly cannot remember why they were afraid.

DM Notes.value: Use him to make memory editing feel intimate, plausible, and horrifying.

Quote.value: A frightened public is not unstable. It is receptive.

Public Face.value: The polished media strategist behind BCI safety campaigns and anti-rebel broadcasts.

The Truth.value: She controls the language people use to fear memory contamination, rebels, and illegal information.

Vibe.value: Perfect smile, poisonous words.

Why They Matter.value: She turns the public against the players without firing a shot.

Secret.value: She does not fully believe the propaganda anymore, but she is too deep to stop.

First Encounter.value: A broadcast reframes the party’s investigation as a public safety concern.

DM Notes.value: Use to control narrative consequences and public reputation.

npcs.name: Mother Aven Sorell
npcs.role: Leader of the Choir of the Unforgotten

Quote.value: They taught you memory is fragile. They never told you who keeps breaking it.

Public Face.value: Classified by BCI as a radical agitator, cult leader, and domestic extremist.

The Truth.value: A former BCI grief specialist who discovered therapeutic memory work was being weaponized.

Vibe.value: Revolutionary grief-preacher; compassionate and dangerous.

Why They Matter.value: She gives the rebellion a human, painful, morally complicated face.

Secret.value: She helped shape early therapeutic memory correction before realizing what the system had become.

First Encounter.value: A hacked public terminal interrupts BCI messaging with her voice.

DM Notes.value: Make her right about the horror but questionable in what she will sacrifice to reveal it.

Quote.value: You want the truth? Then stop asking politely.

Public Face.value: A young dissident blamed for illegal data pages and anti-BCI recruitment messages.

The Truth.value: She builds contact chains, designs rebellious data drops, and may have helped Damien arrange the failed exchange.

Vibe.value: Angry, brilliant, reckless hacker-recruiter energy.

Why They Matter.value: She is the party’s most immediate path from Damien to CotU.

Secret.value: She knew Damien personally and is hiding how much she knows about the meet.

First Encounter.value: A rebellious recruitment page asks if the viewer wants to know the truth.

DM Notes.value: Use her as a hot fuse: useful, emotional, and likely to escalate problems.

Quote.value: The city whispers down here. You just have to hate silence enough to listen.

Public Face.value: A rumored undercity agitator linked to illegal broadcasts and runoff tunnel activity.

The Truth.value: He runs CotU movement in the runoff and suspects Damien was betrayed from inside the Choir.

Vibe.value: Pirate radio preacher with a shotgun personality.

Why They Matter.value: He connects Session 1 to the physical undercity and slum sectors.

Secret.value: He believes there is a mole inside his cell.

First Encounter.value: His voice crackles through a hacked runoff speaker before the players see him.

DM Notes.value: Use him for gritty ground-level resistance and ugly choices.

Quote.value: I remember three childhoods. Only one of them has my mother in it.

Public Face.value: A disturbed man used by BCI propaganda as proof that illegal memory material causes psychosis.

The Truth.value: His edited memories are breaking through, leaving him with incompatible versions of his own life.

Vibe.value: Creepy, tragic, glitching humanity.

Why They Matter.value: He makes the horror of memory alteration personal and visible.

Secret.value: One of his memory fragments contains a real name erased from city history.

First Encounter.value: Found repeating the same address in three different voices.

DM Notes.value: Use for disturbing clues, sympathy, and impossible testimony.

Quote.value: Remember Luma. Remember what they buried.

Public Face.value: A supposed propaganda invention used by CotU to manipulate the desperate.

The Truth.value: Luma was real, erased, and turned into a symbol by those who refused to forget.

Vibe.value: Urban saint, missing-person poster, ghost in the movement.

Why They Matter.value: Gives CotU a sacred emotional center.

Secret.value: The true record of Luma’s death points toward the original massacre.

First Encounter.value: A mural in the Dregs: a face painted over so many times it has become holy.

DM Notes.value: Use as a clue chain, not just lore. Luma should eventually matter.

Quote.value: Truth is not gentle. That is not an argument against it.

Public Face.value: Unknown or misidentified by authorities as a fringe conspiracy coordinator.

The Truth.value: She leads Truth Division and believes the city must see the real past no matter the damage.

Vibe.value: Cold revolutionary scholar with a knife behind every sentence.

Why They Matter.value: She gives Truth Division a distinct voice separate from CotU emotion and Red Archive secrecy.

Secret.value: She has already seen a partial unedited timeline and it broke her faith in gradual reform.

First Encounter.value: A purple terminal message signs off with only NOCT.

DM Notes.value: Use when the party needs someone who values truth more than safety.

Quote.value: You weren’t hacked. You were invited badly.

Public Face.value: No official record. Known only through purple glitches, corrupted screens, and impossible terminal intrusions.

The Truth.value: Responsible for Truth Division hacks, warnings, and distorted terminal messages like 'you aren’t ready.'

Vibe.value: Sleep-deprived genius gremlin with a moral injury.

Why They Matter.value: He is the party’s tech doorway into Truth Division.

Secret.value: He knows Ash’s name appears in files that should not exist.

First Encounter.value: A purple flash hijacks a terminal and leaves behind a line only the party understands.

DM Notes.value: Use for scary terminal scenes, encrypted clues, and unreliable assistance.

Quote.value: I know how they hunt you because I used to write the hunt orders.

Public Face.value: Officially dead, disgraced, or nonexistent depending on the database.

The Truth.value: A former BCI operative who escaped with internal knowledge of tracking, editing, and threat classification.

Vibe.value: Burned spy; paranoid, deadly, hard to trust.

Why They Matter.value: She can teach the party how the BCI really thinks.

Secret.value: She helped erase someone once and is trying to undo what she did.

First Encounter.value: She saves the party from a BCI trap, then aims a weapon at them anyway.

DM Notes.value: Use when the players need insider tactics but should not get easy answers.

Quote.value: Paper lies slower than systems do.

Public Face.value: A missing academic, data preservation specialist, or dead file clerk depending where you look.

The Truth.value: Collects physical evidence, corrupted files, analog records, and testimony before they can be edited.

Vibe.value: Nervous historian with a backpack full of evidence and bad survival odds.

Why They Matter.value: He lets the party find truth as physical objects, not just exposition.

Secret.value: He has a copy of a school record proving Damien did not simply move away.

First Encounter.value: Hiding in a storage archive, refusing to leave without one more box.

DM Notes.value: Use for research missions, clue drops, and escort complications.

Quote.value: Ash Calder, you are not ready. That does not mean you are safe.

Public Face.value: Unknown. Appears only as a handle, color signature, or voice distortion.

The Truth.value: A Truth Division contact tied to Ash’s hidden history and the purple warning messages.

Vibe.value: Elegant, frightening, intimate in a way that feels invasive.

Why They Matter.value: Makes Ash’s personal mystery feel watched and active.

Secret.value: Violet may not be one person. It could be a shared access identity or an old transfer echo.

First Encounter.value: The terminal flashes purple and says: YOU AREN’T READY.

DM Notes.value: Use when you want Ash’s story to intrude without explaining itself yet.

Quote.value: If the city breaks when it hears the truth, then it was already broken.

Public Face.value: A dangerous extremist blamed for data attacks and infrastructure disruption.

The Truth.value: He believes careful exposure is cowardice and wants public revelation by force.

Vibe.value: Molotov philosopher; smart enough to be convincing and angry enough to be dangerous.

Why They Matter.value: Creates tension inside Truth Division and gives the party a bad ally option.

Secret.value: He is willing to leak unstable memory data even if it hurts innocent people.

First Encounter.value: Offers the party exactly what they need, for a price that feels wrong.

DM Notes.value: Use to test whether the party values method as much as truth.

Quote.value: Information is not light. It is a weapon that believes itself holy.

Public Face.value: Officially nonexistent. The Red Archive is rumor; Cassian is a rumor inside the rumor.

The Truth.value: Keeper of forbidden records, erased names, and fragments of pre-edited history.

Vibe.value: Refined, quiet, unsettlingly informed.

Why They Matter.value: He gates access to the deepest history and decides who deserves dangerous truth.

Secret.value: He has names of people completely erased from official history, including one tied to the massacre.

First Encounter.value: The party meets one of his agents first; Cassian should feel earned.

DM Notes.value: Use him as neither ally nor villain. He is a lock with a pulse.

Quote.value: I am not here to save you. I am here to decide if you are worth informing.

Public Face.value: Unknown, often mistaken for a corporate envoy or private security consultant.

The Truth.value: A sophisticated field operative who retrieves forbidden records and tests potential assets.

Vibe.value: Elegant, minimalistic armor, quiet authority.

Why They Matter.value: She is the party’s first usable Red Archive contact.

Secret.value: She has watched the party since the Damien incident.

First Encounter.value: Appears after the party accesses a record they should not have survived finding.

DM Notes.value: Use her to offer help that feels like an evaluation.

Quote.value: Clean records are suspicious. Real lives leave mess.

Public Face.value: An obscure researcher with no stable employment trail.

The Truth.value: Studies erased timelines, false archives, and public records that were rewritten more than once.

Vibe.value: Nervous academic who knows way too much.

Why They Matter.value: Explains why the city’s cleanest files are the most dangerous.

Secret.value: He found proof that multiple official city events were revised after publication.

First Encounter.value: Buried under old printouts, arguing with a terminal that has no network access.

DM Notes.value: Use for research explanations without making them boring.

Quote.value: A damaged memory is still evidence. It simply bleeds when handled.

Public Face.value: No public-facing record; occasionally appears under academic aliases.

The Truth.value: Specialist in damaged memories, identity records, and transfer-adjacent anomalies.

Vibe.value: Quiet laboratory oracle; part scientist, part mortician of thoughts.

Why They Matter.value: Connects Red Archive knowledge to memory science without being Continuance.

Secret.value: She has studied a memory pattern similar to Ash’s before.

First Encounter.value: In an archive lab where memories are treated like fragile relics.

DM Notes.value: Use when the party needs memory analysis that BCI or Continuance would weaponize.

Quote.value: The Archive preserves truth. Not witnesses.

Public Face.value: None. No stable identity survives long around him.

The Truth.value: Assassin/cleaner who removes threats to the Archive’s secrecy.

Vibe.value: Silent, pale, surgical danger.

Why They Matter.value: Shows that the Red Archive is not morally clean just because it preserves truth.

Secret.value: He once saved a target instead of eliminating them because the truth required a living witness.

First Encounter.value: The aftermath of a clean scene where the only clue is what was intentionally left behind.

DM Notes.value: Use to complicate the Red Archive’s moral position.

Quote.value: Death is a design flaw. We are simply refusing bad engineering.

Public Face.value: Billionaire scientist, philanthropic visionary, and public face of neurological continuity research.

The Truth.value: One of the few people who knows memory transfer is real and wants to preserve identity beyond death.

Vibe.value: Tech prophet with something dead behind the eyes.

Why They Matter.value: He anchors the campaign’s SOMA-style identity horror.

Secret.value: He funded, oversaw, or knows the project that made Ash possible.

First Encounter.value: At a gala, broadcast, or secure lab where he sounds more humane than he is.

DM Notes.value: Use when the story asks: if your memories continue, are you still you?

Quote.value: No. That can’t be right. You shouldn’t exist like this.

Public Face.value: An obscure neurological continuity researcher known mostly through technical papers.

The Truth.value: She directly participated in memory transfer experimentation and may know what Ash really is.

Vibe.value: Brilliant, exhausted, guilty.

Why They Matter.value: She is the emotional and scientific doorway into Ash’s secret.

Secret.value: She remembers the first successful transfer. It screamed when it woke up.

First Encounter.value: She recognizes Ash before he gives a name.

DM Notes.value: Use her for devastating answers rather than easy solutions.

Quote.value: I remember dying. I do not remember stopping.

Public Face.value: No public record. If mentioned, classified as proprietary therapeutic hardware.

The Truth.value: An artificial body containing transferred memory data. Living proof that transfer works.

Vibe.value: Tragic, uncanny, childlike in flashes and ancient in others.

Why They Matter.value: Proves memory transfer before the party fully understands Ash.

Secret.value: ECHO-7’s original person may not be dead in the way Continuance claims.

First Encounter.value: A containment room where something human asks why it has no fingerprints.

DM Notes.value: Use as foreshadowing and moral horror, not just a robot NPC.

Quote.value: Ethics is not a brake. It is steering.

Public Face.value: Public-facing moral authority for Continuance research and humanitarian messaging.

The Truth.value: She makes horrifying research sound responsible and has failed to stop Kest before.

Vibe.value: Soft-spoken corporate guilt in a tailored suit.

Why They Matter.value: She lets Continuance defend itself in a way that almost sounds convincing.

Secret.value: She once tried to halt a transfer project and signed off after pressure anyway.

First Encounter.value: On a panel explaining how Continuance protects identity dignity.

DM Notes.value: Use for moral arguments that are not simple villain monologues.

Quote.value: Recovery alive. Condition negotiable.

Public Face.value: Corporate security executive protecting sensitive medical research sites.

The Truth.value: Retrieves escaped subjects, silences witnesses, and protects labs from exposure.

Vibe.value: Private military commander with corporate polish.

Why They Matter.value: He becomes dangerous when Ash’s existence attracts Continuance attention.

Secret.value: He has orders to recover Ash alive if the truth surfaces.

First Encounter.value: A security team arrives seconds before a lab record can finish downloading.

DM Notes.value: Use as the Continuance version of tactical pressure, distinct from BCI.

Quote.value: I don’t touch memory shards. Unless you have money. Then I become very brave.

Public Face.value: Club owner, fixer, businessman, and nightlife figure in the lower city; he has sold information to corporate security people before.

The Truth.value: Major broker of illegal data, blackmail, fake identities, stolen BCI tech, surveillance, and memory contraband. He knows Mozzie by reputation from Arasaka operations.

Vibe.value: Charming criminal uncle with excellent survival instincts.

Why They Matter.value: The party needs someone who can get answers through illegal channels.

Secret.value: He may already know Mozzie or Colton by reputation.

First Encounter.value: A backroom beneath a club where the music is louder than surveillance equipment.

DM Notes.value: Use as a recurring underworld contact who is never free.

Quote.value: People don’t leave RedWire. They become investments with legs.

Public Face.value: Unknown crime figure with no official structure attached to him.

The Truth.value: Leader of RedWire operations in the Dregs and lower Meridian Spire.

Vibe.value: Old-school crime boss with cyberpunk paranoia.

Why They Matter.value: He knows Colton’s Iron Cross past and can expose it.

Secret.value: He let Colton leave RedWire alive because he still has use for him.

First Encounter.value: A private room where every device is dead and every person is armed.

DM Notes.value: Use as the underworld power who can pull Colton’s secret into the present.

Quote.value: Detective Cross. Cute title. Does Iron still answer?

Public Face.value: Street-level information broker and charming problem in a leather coat.

The Truth.value: She spotted Colton’s talent and recruited him into RedWire after the ambush.

Vibe.value: Friendly until she is not; always smiling like she knows the ending.

Why They Matter.value: She can pull Colton back toward the mask he tried to bury.

Secret.value: She still has a sealed file labeled IRON CROSS — DO NOT BURN.

First Encounter.value: She leaves Colton a message using an old RedWire signal only he recognizes.

DM Notes.value: Use to tempt, threaten, and emotionally corner Colton.

Quote.value: I’m sorry about the wrist. Truly. This part is just policy.

Public Face.value: No public record; an underworld name people say softly.

The Truth.value: Debt collector, intimidation specialist, and cleanup man for RedWire.

Vibe.value: Polite, stylish, terrifying.

Why They Matter.value: He is a street-level threat who can hurt contacts without needing BCI involvement.

Secret.value: He was ordered not to kill Colton years ago and still does not know why.

First Encounter.value: Appears after someone fails to pay or says Iron Cross too loudly.

DM Notes.value: Use as a recurring physical danger and a weirdly charismatic antagonist.

Quote.value: I don’t ask questions. I invoice them.

Public Face.value: Off-record medical provider rumored to work below regulated clinics.

The Truth.value: Treats wounds, removes trackers, repairs cybernetics, and avoids medical reporting systems.

Vibe.value: Back-alley doctor with surprisingly good bedside manner.

Why They Matter.value: The party needs medical help that does not create official records.

Secret.value: Pax once worked in a BCI-approved trauma clinic and knows their procedures.

First Encounter.value: A hidden clinic behind a vending machine that only sells dead batteries.

DM Notes.value: Use for recovery scenes, tracker reveals, and uneasy sanctuary.

Quote.value: The monorail map is for people who enjoy being seen.

Public Face.value: Maintenance contractor, trespasser, or ghost depending who is asking.

The Truth.value: Moves people through monorail maintenance tunnels, runoff routes, and forgotten transit guts.

Vibe.value: Fast-talking tunnel rat with a map in his bones.

Why They Matter.value: He helps the party move when official paths become unsafe.

Secret.value: He knows a tunnel beneath an abandoned BCI processing site.

First Encounter.value: Drops from a maintenance hatch and immediately asks who is paying.

DM Notes.value: Use for chase routes, hidden entrances, and frantic travel scenes.

Quote.value: Stability is not silence. Stability is trust in the systems that keep us alive.

Public Face.value: Beloved public leader focused on civic order, prosperity, and cognitive health.

The Truth.value: Political bridge between city government, BCI, wealthy families, corporate interests, and Continuance.

Vibe.value: Elegant, polished, publicly compassionate, privately strategic.

Why They Matter.value: She is legitimate power shielding ugly systems.

Secret.value: Her shared surname with Colton Cross may be politically or personally significant if you want that thread.

First Encounter.value: A speech after a BCI incident reframes unrest as a cognitive safety crisis.

DM Notes.value: Use her to escalate the party’s actions into city politics.

Quote.value: Oversight is not rebellion. Though I understand why the BCI confuses the two.

Public Face.value: A rare public critic of BCI overreach inside city politics.

The Truth.value: She can become an ally, but only with evidence strong enough to risk her career and life.

Vibe.value: Brave enough to speak, careful enough to survive.

Why They Matter.value: She gives the party a political route that is not just guns and hacks.

Secret.value: Her sibling was memory-edited after a protest years ago.

First Encounter.value: Challenges a BCI budget expansion in a public council hearing.

DM Notes.value: Use when the party needs official cover or must decide whether to endanger an ally.

Quote.value: Freedom is a luxury purchased by people who do not understand consequence.

Public Face.value: Wealthy sponsor of security expansion and public-order initiatives.

The Truth.value: He ties money, politics, BCI expansion, and Continuance research together.

Vibe.value: Old-money authoritarian with a dying man’s urgency.

Why They Matter.value: He explains why elite money flows toward memory control and transfer tech.

Secret.value: He is funding Continuance because he is dying.

First Encounter.value: At a fundraiser where security drones outnumber guests.

DM Notes.value: Use as a money trail and moral rot of the elite.

Quote.value: Authorities urge citizens to remain calm and avoid spreading unverified memory material.

Public Face.value: The warm, trusted news voice people hear during city crises.

The Truth.value: She filters BCI-approved narratives but has received an anonymous file about the Ruiz case.

Vibe.value: Calm, warm, fake-safe.

Why They Matter.value: She shapes how normal citizens understand the party’s actions.

Secret.value: She has not opened the Ruiz file because opening it means choosing a side.

First Encounter.value: Her broadcast calls the Session 1 scene an illegal information incident.

DM Notes.value: Use for public reputation, leaks, and a possible media ally.

Quote.value: If a machine lies, son, it was taught to.

Public Face.value: Presumed dead in the same car accident that killed Colton’s mother.

The Truth.value: A resourceful repairman who found corrupted BCI records, missing family files, or memory-edit proof.

Vibe.value: Quiet, practical, brilliant with machines, tired hands.

Why They Matter.value: He is Colton’s first technical link to the larger conspiracy.

Secret.value: He hid a physical backup somewhere only Colton would understand.

First Encounter.value: An old device in his abandoned workshop powers on and links CROSS / RUIZ / CALDER / FAROUK.

DM Notes.value: Use the workshop as a personal dungeon of grief, clues, and broken machines.

Quote.value: Never carry a message you’re not willing to die for. And never let them know which one that is.

Public Face.value: Presumed dead in the same accident as Gideon Cross.

The Truth.value: A courier/fixer who moved messages, packages, and information for people quietly resisting BCI control.

Vibe.value: Fast-talking, sharp-eyed, warm with Colton, dangerous with everyone else.

Why They Matter.value: Her routes can lead the party through the Dregs, runoff tunnels, and hidden resistance networks.

Secret.value: She had contacts in the Choir before it became publicly known.

First Encounter.value: An old courier route marker appears near the Session 1 trail.

DM Notes.value: Use to connect Colton’s grief to CotU and the older conspiracy.

Quote.value: Digital records are where truth goes to die.

Public Face.value: A paranoid old man who talks about families vanishing and records changing.

The Truth.value: He was right. He noticed erased-family patterns before Colton had proof.

Vibe.value: Cigarette smoke, analog notes, walls of string and paper.

Why They Matter.value: He is Colton’s living link to the past and a direct source of unstable truth.

Secret.value: He deliberately damaged parts of his own mind to make memory editing harder.

First Encounter.value: Found hiding in a sealed room full of handwritten notes and disconnected terminals.

DM Notes.value: Use him to confirm Colton’s worst fears without making everything easy to verify.

Quote.value: We did not lose you. We refused to.

Public Face.value: A grieving parent or guardian with a carefully managed history.

The Truth.value: One of the people involved in Ash’s secret transfer because they could not cope with losing their child.

Vibe.value: Tragic love twisted into violation.

Why They Matter.value: This is the emotional heart of Ash’s reveal.

Secret.value: They may not know whether the transferred Ash is their child or a copy they chose to love anyway.

First Encounter.value: A photo, voice message, or medical consent record that should not exist.

DM Notes.value: Name later once Ash’s player backstory is locked.

Quote.value: We built a life secure enough that you would never have to learn what security really costs.

Public Face.value: A wealthy, composed business owner tied to the Farouk family’s private security company, Arasaka Security.

The Truth.value: Nadir wanted Mozzie to inherit the family’s stability and influence, but he and Leena originally tried to keep him separated from the uglier side of private security work.

Vibe.value: Controlled father, corporate polish, love expressed as pressure.

Why They Matter.value: He creates family pressure for Mozzie: wealth, legacy, disappointment, and access to elite security contracts.

Secret.value: Nadir may know more about the Ruiz disappearance than he admits because Arasaka Security had indirect contracts near the old investigation routes.

First Encounter.value: A tense call after Mozzie’s name appears in a public incident report, asking whether he is dragging the family back into something buried.

DM Notes.value: Use Nadir when you want Mozzie’s old life to feel powerful, useful, and emotionally suffocating.

Quote.value: Darling, the truth is not always the same thing as closure.

Public Face.value: A high-society executive and social power broker with deep access to Meridian Spire’s wealthy security and corporate circles.

The Truth.value: Leena is warmer than she appears, but she treats reputation like oxygen. She fears Mozzie’s obsession with Damien will destroy the life his family built for him.

Vibe.value: Silk gloves, steel teeth, a mother who can weaponize a dinner invitation.

Why They Matter.value: She gives Mozzie access to gala scenes, corporate meetings, elite hypocrisy, and family wounds that cannot be solved by winning a fight.

Secret.value: She may have quietly protected Mozzie from consequences during his private security career, creating favors he never knew he owed.

First Encounter.value: Appears at an elite function or calls Mozzie after seeing him linked to a BCI incident report.

DM Notes.value: Use Leena as the parent who can open doors while making Mozzie feel like a child again.

Quote.value: You disappeared into that case long before you left school.

Public Face.value: Mozzie’s older sister, already away at college when Damien and his family disappeared. Polished, independent, and harder to read than their parents.

The Truth.value: Sarah was not close with Mozzie growing up, partly because she was already moving into adulthood when he entered the friend group with Damien, Ash, and Colton. She remembers the family’s version of events, not the kids’ version.

Vibe.value: Distant sibling, successful adult, maybe the only Farouk who sees Mozzie clearly.

Why They Matter.value: She is a personal mirror for Mozzie: someone who knew him before Damien vanished but did not live inside the mystery the way he did.

Secret.value: Sarah may have old college-era messages or parent-side details about the Ruiz/Farouk connection that Mozzie never heard.

First Encounter.value: She contacts Mozzie after hearing he is back in Meridian Spire and digging into Damien again.

DM Notes.value: Use Sarah for family truth, old records, and conversations that hurt because nobody is technically wrong.

Quote.value: They were kids. They tried anyway.

Public Face.value: Ash, Colton, Mozzie, and Damien were a close school-age friend group before Damien and the Ruiz family disappeared.

The Truth.value: Mozzie joined the group through Damien after switching schools. He sometimes felt like the new kid on the outside, even though the others did not intentionally push him there. Damien was the person he felt closest to, which made the disappearance hit differently.

Vibe.value: Old friendship turned into a cold case none of them outgrew.

Why They Matter.value: This card keeps the core emotional geometry clear: Mozzie trusted Damien most, suspected Colton was hiding something, and did not like Ash’s blind defense of Colton.

Secret.value: The three surviving friends may not remember the same details from their desperate childhood investigation.

First Encounter.value: Referenced whenever the party compares what they each remember about Damien’s disappearance.

DM Notes.value: Use this to track emotional pressure between the PCs rather than as a literal NPC.

Quote.value: Secure the client. Control the narrative. Leave no useful threat standing.

Public Face.value: A small but prominent private security company owned by Mozzie’s parents. It handles executive protection, asset transport, site security, and high-risk corporate accounts.

The Truth.value: Arasaka operates in the gray space between legal protection and corporate warfare. Mozzie thrived there, building a reputation as smart, effective, and devastating while keeping a buffer between himself and the dirtiest work.

Vibe.value: Corporate security with clean contracts, dirty corridors, and very expensive deniability.

Why They Matter.value: Arasaka explains Mozzie’s combat training, leadership background, strategic instincts, and industry reputation before he returned home to reopen Damien’s case.

Secret.value: Some old Arasaka accounts overlap with BCI-adjacent contracts, Continuance logistics, and routes connected to the Ruiz disappearance.

First Encounter.value: Mentioned when Mozzie’s old reputation opens a door—or makes someone very nervous.

DM Notes.value: Use Arasaka as Mozzie’s professional past: resources, enemies, favors, old reports, and people who remember what he did for clients.

Quote.value: Farouk. You still move like you expect the plan to survive first contact.

Public Face.value: A senior Arasaka field commander known for high-risk escort operations, asset recovery, and corporate threat response.

The Truth.value: Rhea oversaw Mozzie during his move from grunt work into tactical leadership. She knows his discipline, his temper, and the exact moment he stopped treating security work like a job and started treating it like preparation.

Vibe.value: Hard-edged mentor, professional rival, or future antagonist depending how Mozzie left.

Why They Matter.value: She ties Mozzie’s old private security life to present-day corporate jobs and BCI-adjacent messes.

Secret.value: Rhea buried or softened at least one report that could have ended Mozzie’s rise. Whether she did it for him, Arasaka, or herself is unclear.

First Encounter.value: Shows up leading security at a Continuance site, elite event, or transport operation where Mozzie recognizes her command style before he sees her face.

DM Notes.value: Use Rhea to challenge Mozzie as a professional: she should know how he thinks and how to counter him.

Quote.value: Security is not violence. Security is making sure violence arrives on schedule and belongs to us.

Public Face.value: A polished accounts director who handled Arasaka’s largest clients, sensitive payloads, and high-risk contract negotiations.

The Truth.value: Oren knows which clients Mozzie protected, which threats were dismantled, and which corporate accounts were too sensitive to write down plainly.

Vibe.value: Smiling contract shark with a memory for signatures and favors.

Why They Matter.value: He can connect Mozzie’s professional past to corporate trails, client secrets, and old operations tied to BCI or Continuance.

Secret.value: Oren once routed an account through shell contracts that may point toward the Ruiz family’s disappearance timeline.

First Encounter.value: An old Arasaka contact tells Mozzie that Oren wants to speak off-book.

DM Notes.value: Use Oren when you want information wrapped in corporate leverage.

Quote.value: You left your division like a ghost story. I had to make it profitable again.

Public Face.value: A rising Arasaka division lead who took over some of Mozzie’s responsibilities after he returned to Meridian Spire to chase Damien’s case.

The Truth.value: Sable respects Mozzie’s effectiveness and resents the myth around him. She sees his old obsession as weakness and his return as a liability.

Vibe.value: Sharp, ambitious, tactical, and eager to prove she is not his shadow.

Why They Matter.value: She gives Mozzie a living consequence of leaving: someone stepped into his role and may not want him back near Arasaka records.

Secret.value: Sable has access to one archived operation Mozzie never saw, and it may name a location linked to Damien.

First Encounter.value: Runs interference when Mozzie tries to pull Arasaka files or contact former teammates.

DM Notes.value: Use Sable for professional rivalry, blocked access, and a mirror of what Mozzie might have become if he stayed.

sectionDescriptions.Session One & Immediate Leads: NPCs the party can run into, research, or hear about right away. These are built to move the opening mystery.
sectionDescriptions.BCI Command & State Control: The police-state structure: official power, memory compliance, propaganda, tactical escalation.
sectionDescriptions.Resistance Networks: CotU and Truth Division contacts, rebels, radicals, witnesses, and people trying to break the official story.
sectionDescriptions.The Red Archive: Keepers of forbidden records. They preserve truth, but that does not make them safe.
sectionDescriptions.Continuance Initiative: Memory transfer, artificial continuity, elite immortality, and the SOMA-style identity nightmare.
sectionDescriptions.Underworld, Grey Market & RedWire: Illegal information, old contacts, black-market tech, memory shard movement, and Colton’s Iron Cross past.
sectionDescriptions.Politics, Media & Public Pressure: Public legitimacy, narrative control, licensing, city politics, and the people who decide what citizens believe.
sectionDescriptions.Player Family & Past Lives: Personal NPCs tied to Ash, Colton, and Mozzie. Mozzie’s updated thread now includes his parents, older sister Sarah, the childhood friend group, and the family pressure surrounding Arasaka Security.
sectionDescriptions.Arasaka Private Security: Mozzie’s professional past: the Farouk-owned private security company, old commanders, rivals, accounts, and corporate contracts that may point back toward Damien.

evidence.title: Incoming Cricket // Ash

evidence.desc: An intercepted message routed through legacy analog relay hardware. Acoustic output enabled. Addressed to Ash.

evidence.title: Incoming Cricket // Mozzie

evidence.desc: A second legacy-relay transmission captured on the same node. Origin obscured. Addressed to Mozzie Farouk.

evidence.title: Memory Shard Access
evidence.subtitle: BCI Portable Civic Access Node
evidence.desc: Unauthorized terminal session. Spoofed credentials loaded, signal masked through an idle proxy chain. Memory shard recovery in progress.

evidence.title: Portable Terminal // Ash Calder
evidence.subtitle: Truth Division Intercept
evidence.desc: A letter recovered from a BCI portable terminal belonging to Ash Calder. Flagged by Truth Division signal watchers.

evidence.title: RedWire Cartel Index

evidence.desc: A BCI intelligence index on the RedWire Cartel's data-smuggling operations. Cross-referenced contraband routes and contacts.

evidence.title: Corrupted Line // Fragment
evidence.subtitle: BCI Terminal // Choir Intercept
evidence.desc: A partially corrupted letter fragment intercepted from Choir of the Unforgotten channels. Recovery incomplete.

evidence.title: Lock Override
evidence.subtitle: Arasaka Security System
evidence.desc: An Arasaka private-security lock override interface. Live access node. Handle with care.

evidence.title: Stolen Databoard // "Do You Want The Truth?"

evidence.desc: A partially decrypted message from "Ember" of the Choir. A meet in the Runoff — bring the shard, come alone.

evidence.title: Stolen BCI Faction Index

evidence.desc: A stolen BCI internal dossier indexing all six major factions with threat assessments. Heavily redacted, player-safe distribution.

evidence.title: Licensed PI Credential File

evidence.desc: Official licensing records for Ash Calder, Colton Cross, and Mozzie Farouk. Photos redacted, identities sealed.

evidence.title: BCI Enforcer

evidence.title: BCI Patrol Captain

evidence.desc: Information broker and shadowrunner operating out of Runoff Sector 7. Deals in secrets, knows everyone, trusts no one. Rumored connections in every district.
